intent: A single spacecraft on a near-circular low-Earth orbit (low eccentricity) at 28.5 deg inclination, propagated for roughly one orbit, with a ReportFile recording its radial distance from Earth.
request: Create a spacecraft in a circular low-Earth orbit at 28.5 degrees inclination, propagate for one revolution, and report its distance from Earth.

intent: A spacecraft using an Earth gravity (primary-body) force model, propagated for about one day, with a ReportFile recording the semi-major axis.
request: Set up a spacecraft with an Earth point-mass gravity model, propagate for one day, and report the semi-major axis.

intent: A targeted impulsive maneuver solved by a DifferentialCorrector — vary the burn to achieve a target apoapsis radius — then report the achieved radius.
request: Use a differential corrector to find the maneuver that raises the orbit to a target apogee radius, then report the achieved radius.

intent: A targeted impulsive maneuver solved by a DifferentialCorrector — vary the burn to achieve a (far) target apoapsis radius — then report the achieved radius.
request: Use a differential corrector to drive the orbit's apogee radius to a far target and report the achieved radius.
